title: Sculpt the valley
intro: Reading the landscape was step one — now BUILD it. Three balls release from the flags on the left. They should reach the home basin on the right, but a decoy valley keeps stealing them. You cannot dig: negative charges make holes, and holes CAPTURE balls. Instead you get positive charges — hills. Place ridges to bend the watershed so all three release points drain home. Fewer ridges, more stars.
explain: You just did potential engineering: superposition means every charge's landscape simply ADDS, so a positive charge is a hill you can put anywhere, and a hill upstream redirects entire watersheds without touching the ball. Notice what failed if you tried placing charges as stepping stones — anything attractive becomes a trap, because rolling downhill ends at the FIRST minimum, not the nicest one. Real devices are sculpted exactly this way: dopant ions implanted into silicon are fixed charges whose only job is to reshape the energy landscape that electrons roll through. You will fire those dopants yourself in Chapter 6.
